description_long: This is a tall, lattice-structured metal tower with a square base that tapers as it rises. The structure features multiple circular dish antennas mounted at various heights and orientations along its vertical frame, suggesting its use for telecommunications or power transmission. The upper section includes a fenced platform, likely for maintenance access, and a small vertical pole or lightning rod extending from the top. The lattice framework is composed of crisscrossed metal beams, providing both strength and stability while minimizing wind resistance. The tower is predominantly metallic gray, with some of the dish antennas appearing darker in color. No visible warning signage or high-voltage equipment is present, and the structure is clearly designed to be a fixed, non-movable installation.
description: A tall, metallic lattice tower with multiple circular dish antennas and a fenced platform at the top. The structure is primarily gray and features a sturdy, crisscrossed metal framework.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the tower, with several dish antennas visible on both sides and the fenced platform clearly seen at the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the staggered arrangement of the dish antennas, with the lattice structure and platform still visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite side of the tower, with dish antennas distributed vertically and the lattice framework prominent.
description_view_3: Alternate side view showing the dish antennas from a different angle, emphasizing the open lattice design and the top platform.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: No traditional crossarms are present; instead, multiple circular dish antennas are mounted at staggered heights along the vertical frame.
attribute_configuration_of_attached_powe: No power lines are visibly attached; the structure is equipped with multiple dish antennas arranged vertically and laterally.
